item.title: HackMIT 2014
item.dc:creator: Gautam
item.description:
Highlights Met Alexis Ohanian (again) and Stephen Wolfram! Hack: Yo to your coffee machine to start brewing your coffee, and receive a Yo back when it’s done. Hosted by Pi Lambda Phi for a night. “What exactly are you going to hack into?” — Immigration officer

The post HackMIT 2014 appeared first on Gautam Gupta.
item.content:encoded:
Highlights

  • Met Alexis Ohanian (again) and Stephen Wolfram!
  • Hack: Yo to your coffee machine to start brewing your coffee, and receive a Yo back when it’s done.
  • Hosted by Pi Lambda Phi for a night.
  • “What exactly are you going to hack into?” — Immigration officer

The post HackMIT 2014 appeared first on Gautam Gupta.

item.title: YHack 2014 (Yale)
item.dc:creator: Gautam
item.description:
Highlights Beautiful university. Met Betsy, a humble person and a student at Yale who showed us around and was really helpful! Hard to find people like her. Met Edric Tam, a student at John Hopkins. Dare to beat his intelligence. Slept in a Yale residence lounge. Read up on Bitcoins and cryptocurrencies. Unfortunately couldn’t come… Continue reading YHack 2014 (Yale)

The post YHack 2014 (Yale) appeared first on Gautam Gupta.
item.content:encoded:
Highlights

  • Beautiful university.
  • Met Betsy, a humble person and a student at Yale who showed us around and was really helpful! Hard to find people like her.
  • Met Edric Tam, a student at John Hopkins. Dare to beat his intelligence.
  • Slept in a Yale residence lounge.
  • Read up on Bitcoins and cryptocurrencies. Unfortunately couldn’t come up with a hack.

The post YHack 2014 (Yale) appeared first on Gautam Gupta.

item.title: Trip to Googleplex No. 2!
item.dc:creator: Gautam
item.description:
Hey there! As you might have read on my blog, I got another chance to visit the Googleplex as a part of my Google Code-in achievement. But this time, I went with both my parents and to the east coast as well! Google was kind enough to give us an option to make our trips… Continue reading Trip to Googleplex No. 2!

The post Trip to Googleplex No. 2! appeared first on Gautam Gupta.
item.content:encoded:
Hey there! As you might have read on my blog, I got another chance to visit the Googleplex as a part of my Google Code-in achievement. But this time, I went with both my parents and to the east coast as well! Google was kind enough to give us an option to make our trips flexible since it’s once in a lifetime opportunity (though has struck me two times :P). And as you might have imagined, Google gave us a lot of swag too – including a brand new Galaxy Nexus! Some of the grand prize winners including me also made plans to visit other companies to meet Silicon Valley’s finest developers and get a sense of how it is to work there. I also got to meet my friend @kunalbhalla, who works at Facebook. Pictures follow.

Response code is 404

The post Trip to Googleplex No. 2! appeared first on Gautam Gupta.

item.title: Trip to Googleplex
item.dc:creator: Gautam
item.description:
Helloe folks! Did I tell you about my Google Code-in achievement? As a part of the prize, I also got a chance to visit the Googleplex in Mountain View, California! Yay! And I was there from June 5th to 11th and had loads of fun touring the Googleplex, talking to Googlers, getting Google Goodies including… Continue reading Trip to Googleplex

The post Trip to Googleplex appeared first on Gautam Gupta.
item.content:encoded:
Helloe folks! Did I tell you about my Google Code-in achievement? As a part of the prize, I also got a chance to visit the Googleplex in Mountain View, California! Yay! And I was there from June 5th to 11th and had loads of fun touring the Googleplex, talking to Googlers, getting Google Goodies including the Bugdroid, buying Google Schwag and what not.

The trip to Google was one of the most memorable and happiest moments of my life. I got to meet all the intelligent high-school coders from around the world, meet all the amazing Googlers and learn from them, receive a beautiful (but heavy :P) trophy, have a lunch at Charlie’s Cafe at Google HQ, hack around the Google buildings, clicking pictures in photography-prohibited areas (well, kidding ;)), getting the Google Goodies (which included bottles, chocolates, pens, notebooks, books, stickers, big android plushie aka bugdroid, souvenirs, shirts, jackets and a blanket), buying Google Schwag (mouse, torch, key chain, USB port hub and other geeky stuff), having wonderful dinners at restaurants, touring the California Academy of Sciences and riding those cool Segways. It all amounted to be a whole ton of fun and enjoyment, but wouldn’t of course not have been possible without the efforts and hard work of Carol, Cat, Stephanie and the whole Google team, to which I salute!

A picture says a thousand words and, I have 100 pictures uploaded on Picasa. That amounts to 100,000 words! OMG! Here they are!

The post Trip to Googleplex appeared first on Gautam Gupta.

item.title: Capturing the Lightning!
item.dc:creator: Gautam
item.description:
There were lightnings in front of my house (I live on eighth floor) and my Sony DSC-W320 was lying beside me, so I just took it, switched on the burst mode and started clicking. I don’t have a tripod so I just did it with my elbows resting on my desk and capturing through the… Continue reading Capturing the Lightning!

The post Capturing the Lightning! appeared first on Gautam Gupta.
item.content:encoded:
There were lightnings in front of my house (I live on eighth floor) and my Sony DSC-W320 was lying beside me, so I just took it, switched on the burst mode and started clicking. I don’t have a tripod so I just did it with my elbows resting on my desk and capturing through the window. Here are the pictures.

If you want to know how to capture lightnings, then you may follow this tutorial (I read that article afterwards) – but I really like the pictures posted there.

The post Capturing the Lightning! appeared first on Gautam Gupta.
